famous quote:
"God has not been trying an experiment on my faith or love in order to find out their quality. He knew it already. It was I who didn't. In this trial He makes us occupy the dock, the witness box, and the bench all at once. He always knew that my temple was a house of cards. His only way of making me realize the fact was to knock it down."
-- C.S. Lewis (A Grief Observed)
Interview Text:
Dena:

I went to Seminary with Dena.

When I interviewed her Dena has felt God in several ways, some miraculous.
 
Dena's 29 year old Daughter, Bailee had a stroke this year. But then complications led to a brain bleed and they sustained her on life support for 3 days while they prepared to transplant her organs. 

Dena says, "We had to wait with her dead-but-undead body for 3 days while they arranged the organ donor situation. By chance, Bailee's husband had been working in that hospital, in that very wing, as a chaplain for his internship. The other chaplains knew him. The nurses and doctors knew him. This meant we had loving people with us the whole time. We were bathed in constant prayer. The whole staff knew our family. They did what they call an 'honor walk' where the staff lined the halls as they wheeled Bailee's body to have her organs donated."

Dena notes that it is also coincidental that it was on the third day that Bailee went to give her organs to live on in other people. It was also a week or two before Easter, the holiday about rebirth. Dena said the way they felt God the most during this time was through the constant presence of all the people that were there loving them. They received an abundance of meals, prayers and visits.
Music Video Instructions: Use this as inspiration to pray again. Say what you've been needing to say to God.
